name: El Miro de Rabón y Pico Nevadín – trail running circular desde Rabanal de Arriba (Espacio Natural de los Ancares Leoneses y Alto Sil)
region_intro: Jogging around Villablino offers routes through the mountainous terrain of the Ancares Leoneses and Alto Sil regions. The landscape is characterized by significant elevation changes, with trails often leading through valleys, past peaks, and alongside natural features. These running routes provide opportunities to explore the rugged natural environment of the area. Best jogging routes around Villablino The most popular jogging route is El Miro de Rabón y Pico Nevadín – trail running circular desde Rabanal de Arriba (Espacio Natural de los Ancares Leoneses y Alto Sil), a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through a protected natural space. Another top favourite among local runners is Brañas de la Seita, Zaramedo, and Degollada Loop from Palacios del Sil, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path. This circular route explores the natural surroundings of Palacios del Sil, offering varied terrain. Local runners also love the El Cabril Route, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Jogging around Villablino is defined by mountainous trails, significant elevation changes, and routes through natural parks. The network offers options primarily for experienced runners seeking challenging terrain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 150 runners have used komoot to explore Villablino's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of running routes around Villablino?
A: The running routes around Villablino are predominantly challenging. Out of 11 available routes, 10 are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and mountainous terrain. These trails are best suited for experienced runners seeking a demanding workout.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in Villablino?
A: Most running routes in the Villablino area are rated as difficult due to the mountainous landscape and substantial elevation gains. There are no routes specifically classified as easy, and only one is considered moderate. Beginners should be prepared for challenging conditions and consider shorter sections or alternative activities.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Villablino?
A: You can expect rugged, mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes. The routes often traverse valleys, ascend peaks, and wind through natural features within the Ancares Leoneses and Alto Sil regions. Trails are generally unpaved and can be demanding.

Q: What are some scenic points or landmarks I might see while running in Villablino?
A: The region offers stunning natural beauty. Along your runs, you might encounter impressive mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5371946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Leitariegos&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6856440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Cerredo&lt;/a&gt;. Summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moro de Rabón (1,983 m)&lt;/a&gt; also offer panoramic views. Some routes pass through traditional settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5539368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Somiedo&lt;/a&gt; or historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6332683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Braña de Mumián&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055958413/branas-de-la-seita-zaramedo-and-degollada-loop-from-palacios-del-sil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brañas de la Seita, Zaramedo, and Degollada Loop from Palacios del Sil&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option exploring the natural surroundings. Another is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055952216/el-miro-de-rabon-y-pico-nevadin-trail-running-circular-desde-rabanal-de-arriba-espacio-natural-de-los-ancares-leoneses-y-alto-sil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Miro de Rabón y Pico Nevadín – trail running circular desde Rabanal de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Villablino?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running in Villablino, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be good for higher altitude trails where it's cooler, but be prepared for sun exposure. Winter running is possible but requires appropriate gear for snow and ice, especially on higher passes.

Q: Are the running trails in Villablino suitable for families with children?
A: Given that most routes are classified as difficult with significant elevation changes, the trails around Villablino are generally not ideal for families with young children or those seeking very easy walks. They are more suited for experienced runners or hikers. For family-friendly activities, you might consider shorter, less strenuous paths in nearby villages or nature areas.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Many natural areas in Spain, including those around Villablino, permit dogs on trails, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and livestock. Always check local regulations for specific natural parks or protected areas you plan to visit. Ensure your dog is fit enough for challenging, mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' running routes in the area?
A: While many routes offer stunning views, a less frequently mentioned but equally rewarding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Valley – El Veneiru Teito Hut loop from Rio del Valle&lt;/a&gt;. This extensive 40.9 km route offers a deep dive into the region's natural beauty, ideal for those looking for a truly immersive and challenging experience away from more popular spots.

Q: What is the typical duration for a running route in Villablino?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277761002/el-cabril-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cabril Route&lt;/a&gt;, at 14.4 km, typically takes around 2 hours 34 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Somiedo – Braña de Mumián loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 30 km, can take over 4 hours.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Villablino?
A: Runners using komoot frequently praise the challenging nature and the stunning natural landscapes of the Villablino region. They often highlight the significant elevation changes and the immersive experience of running through protected natural spaces, making it a favorite for those seeking demanding trail runs.
tips: From the summit of El Moro de Rabón, we have spectacular views of the surrounding area, as well as the nearby Pico Nevadín and a large part of the Sierra de Gistredo.
Beautiful path that runs along the crest of the mountain between Miro de Rabón (1,983 m) and Boqueta de Fuentefría (1,895 m). He offers us a postcard stamp.

Beautiful 360 degree views that we find in the Pico Nevadín, located at 2,077 meters above sea level. On clear days we will have a privileged view of the Catoute and Cernella range, as well as other peaks to the south such as Valdeiglesias and Tambarón.

waypoints.name: Crest Trail Between Miro de Rabón and Boqueta de Fuentefría
